declined.claim: OpenAI expanded its revolving credit facility to approximately $4.7 billion.
OpenAI expects to soon reach 1 billion weekly active users, which would be the fastest platform to achieve that milestone.

OpenAI's revolving credit facility is supported by JPMorgan Chase, Citi, Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, Wells Fargo, Mizuho, Royal Bank of Canada, SMBC, UBS, HSBC, and Santander, and remains undrawn at close.
OpenAI will be included in several ARK Invest ETFs, broadening ownership to retail investors.
Anthropic closed a $13 billion Series F funding round in September 2025, valuing it at $183 billion.
Claude Pro consumer subscription is $20 per month.
Claude Max subscription is $100/month or $200/month for heavy users.
Claude Team subscription is $30 per user per month with a 5-seat minimum.
Cognizant rolled out Claude tools internally to up to 350,000 associates.
Deloitte announced its largest-ever enterprise deployment of Anthropic's Claude across more than 470,000 employees in 150 countries in October 2025.

raw_pool.claim: Over $3 billion was raised from individual investors through bank channels in this round.
ChatGPT has over 50 million subscribers.
OpenAI's infrastructure now spans cloud providers Microsoft, Oracle, AWS, CoreWeave, and Google Cloud.
OpenAI's silicon strategy spans NVIDIA, AMD, AWS Trainium, Cerebras, and a custom chip developed in partnership with Broadcom.
OpenAI's data center partnerships span Oracle, SBE, and SoftBank.
Over the past 15 months, OpenAI has expanded its infrastructure strategy beyond a small number of core providers.
Nvidia remains the foundation of OpenAI's infrastructure, powering the training fleet and majority of the inference stack.
NVIDIA reported 31% revenue growth.
NVIDIA reported 200 basis points of gross margin growth.
NVIDIA reported 850 basis points of operating margin growth.
NVIDIA identifies 4 multi-billion dollar GPU computing growth drivers: Gaming, VR, AI, and Self-Driving Cars.
AMD reported record revenue and earnings for full year 2025, described as 'a defining year for AMD'.
AMD and Meta announced an expanded strategic partnership to deploy 6 gigawatts of AMD GPUs.
AMD and OpenAI announced a strategic partnership to deploy 6 gigawatts of AMD GPUs.
AMD unveiled a strategy to lead the $1 trillion compute market at its Financial Analyst Day on November 11, 2025.
AMD completed the divestiture of ZT Systems Data Center Infrastructure Manufacturing Business to Sanmina on October 27, 2025.
AMD reported Q3 2025 financial results on November 4, 2025, with strong demand cited for high-performance and AI platforms.
AMD reported Q2 2025 financial results on August 5, 2025.
AMD reported Q4 and full year 2025 financial results on February 3, 2026, citing record revenue and earnings.
AMD's data center AI franchise is described as 'rapidly scaling' entering 2026, with accelerating adoption of EPYC and Ryzen CPUs.
AMD and TCS announced collaboration to bring 'Helios' rack-scale AI architecture to India.
AMD and Eviden partnered to power Europe's new exascale supercomputer, the first based in France.
AMD powered Frontier AI training for Zyphra, indicating AI training infrastructure deployment.
AMD and Cohere expanded global AI collaboration to power enterprise and sovereign deployments with AMD AI infrastructure.
AMD powered U.S. Sovereign AI Factory Supercomputers, accelerating an open American AI stack.
AMD and HPE expanded collaboration to advance open rack-scale AI infrastructure.
Anthropic revenue was $9B at the end of 2025.
Anthropic revenue grew approximately 1,400% year-over-year as of March 2026.
Business customers account for approximately 80% of Anthropic revenue as of October 2025.
The number of customers spending over $100,000 annually on Claude has grown 7x in the past year.
Over 500 customers now spend over $1 million annually on Claude, up from a dozen two years ago.
Eight of the Fortune 10 are now Claude customers.
Claude Code hit $2.5B in annualized revenue in February 2026, more than doubling since the beginning of 2026.
Anthropic closed a $30 billion Series G funding round on February 12, 2026, at a $380 billion post-money valuation.
Amazon has invested $8 billion total in Anthropic.
Microsoft and Nvidia were contributing up to $15 billion to Anthropic's February 2026 financing round.
Anthropic's Series F valuation was $183 billion in September 2025, up from $61.5B in March 2025.
Anthropic's valuation was $61.5B in March 2025 (Series E).
Anthropic's total funding raised is $6.98B as of 2024 per Sacra's summary figure.
Amazon is investing an additional $5 billion in Anthropic (April 2026 announced round).
Claude Opus 4.6 is priced at $5 per million input tokens and $25 per million output tokens.
Claude Opus 4.6 extended context pricing is $10 per million input tokens and $37.50 per million output tokens.
Claude Sonnet 4.5 API is priced at $3 per million input tokens and $15 per million output tokens.
Claude Sonnet 4.5 long-context prompts over 200K tokens are priced at $6 per million input tokens and $22.50 per million output tokens.
Batch processing for Claude APIs offers 50% savings versus standard pricing.
Claude Sonnet is up to 80% cheaper per token compared to GPT-4.
Subscriptions account for 10-15% of Anthropic's total revenue.
Anthropic expects to burn approximately $3B in cash in 2025, down from $5.6B the prior year.
Anthropic faces an estimated $80B in cloud infrastructure costs through 2029, up dramatically from roughly $3B the prior year.
Claude Code has reached 29 million daily installs on VS Code.
Claude Code accounts for approximately 4% of all public GitHub commits.
Claude Code benchmarks at 72.5% on SWE-bench.
Anthropic and Snowflake expanded their partnership with a $200 million multi-year agreement in December 2025.
In September 2025, Microsoft began integrating Anthropic's models into Office 365 Copilot, potentially reaching more than 100 million users.
Anthropic and Google announced a cloud partnership granting access to up to one million TPUs and adding over a gigawatt of compute capacity by 2026.
Anthropic commits $50 billion to data center upgrades (November 2025 announcement).
